title: tmux yank to system clipboard not reaching Blink (iOS) over SSH
description:
SYMPTOM: vim yank in tmux reaches iOS Blink system clipboard, but tmux copy-mode yank (y) does NOT.

DIAGNOSIS so far:
- Config ~/Code/dotfiles/.tmux.conf is correct: 'set -s set-clipboard on', terminal-features xterm-256color:clipboard (live-confirmed), 'allow-passthrough all', y bound to copy-pipe.
- vim works because it wraps OSC52 in tmux passthrough DCS -> raw bytes to Blink, bypassing tmux's own clipboard logic.
- tmux NATIVE OSC52 path is dead: 'tmux load-buffer -w -' did NOT update Blink clipboard.
- Hypothesis: current Blink client negotiated terminal-features at attach time BEFORE clipboard feature was active, so tmux flags client as non-clipboard-capable and silently skips OSC52 emission.

NEXT STEP (untested): reattach client to re-negotiate.
  1. detach (prefix C-h, then d)
  2. tmux attach   (or 'tmux kill-server' then restart for clean re-negotiation)
  3. TEST in Blink shell directly: tmux load-buffer -w - <<< 'REATTACH_TEST'  then long-press Paste.

IF still failing after clean reattach: route copy-mode y through passthrough like vim. Edit line ~92 of .tmux.conf to copy-pipe-and-cancel that base64-encodes selection and writes OSC52 wrapped in tmux passthrough DCS to /dev/tty.

NOTE: ignore the old pbcopy/reattach-to-user-namespace binding (line 91) - that targets the Mac clipboard, useless for iOS Blink.
notes:
CORRECTED ROOT CAUSE + FIX 2026-05-30 (supersedes the earlier "delete the Ms line / rely on native OSC52" conclusion, which was WRONG — native tmux OSC52 never works on Blink):

REAL ROOT CAUSE (two layers):
1. The original Ms terminal-override was malformed (double-escaped \\E/\\7 -> literal backslash-E), which suppressed emission. That part of the prior diagnosis held.
2. BUT deleting it was the wrong fix. Proven empirically (isolated tmux 3.6b + pty capture, every escaping variant): tmux's native set-clipboard path ALWAYS emits OSC52 with an EMPTY selection field (]52;;) and IGNORES any Ms terminal-override entirely (hardcoding 'c' in the override still produced ]52;;). So no slash-fix can ever make the native path emit ]52;c;.
3. iOS Blink REQUIRES the explicit 'c' selection field. Confirmed on REAL Blink (outside tmux, raw printf): printf '\033]52;c;<b64>\007' pastes + shows the scissors icon; printf '\033]52;;<b64>\007' (empty) is silently dropped. That is exactly why vim works (vim emits ]52;c; via passthrough) and tmux copy-mode didn't.

FIX APPLIED to ~/Code/dotfiles/.tmux.conf (working tree, uncommitted):
- set -s set-clipboard off  (was 'on') — stops tmux emitting the useless empty ]52;; on copy.
- copy-mode-vi 'y' rebound to copy-pipe-and-cancel that base64s the selection and emits
  \033Ptmux;\033\033]52;c;<b64>\007\033\\  to #{pane_tty}. I.e. OSC52 with explicit 'c',
  wrapped in tmux passthrough DCS, written to the pane tty so tmux unwraps and forwards it
  to the outer terminal (Blink). Mirrors vim's proven-on-Blink path.
- IMPORTANT: must write to #{pane_tty}, NOT /dev/tty — the copy-pipe job is detached, so
  /dev/tty doesn't reach the client (verified: /dev/tty -> no emission; #{pane_tty} -> works).
- allow-passthrough all (already present) is required for the DCS unwrap.

VERIFIED Mac-side end-to-end: isolated tmux 3.6b, real config, drove copy-mode + 'y' via a pty
client; captured exactly ONE sequence: \x1b]52;c;Q09QWU1FCg==\x07 (= "COPYME\n"). Config parses clean.

REMAINING: live reload + paste-on-iPhone confirmation. Reload (no kill-server needed — bind + set
overwrite cleanly): prefix C-h then r  (or tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf). Then in Blink tmux:
i (copy-mode) -> v -> select -> y, then long-press Paste. Expect paste + scissors icon.

RESOLVED 2026-05-30: user confirmed paste works on iPhone Blink after reload. Fix verified end-to-end on real hardware.
